selector.exact: always been the shallowest and most worthless of the disciplines of philosophy

annotations.anchor: the shallowest and most worthless of the disciplines of philosophy
annotations.note: The chapter opens by demolishing 'scientific' psychology: it fails, Spengler says, because the soul is not a systematic object that reason can dissect into elements and functions. What psychology really produces is a 'soul-image' — a picture the soul makes of itself, always coloured by the Culture doing the picturing. Read the chapter as a morphology of these soul-images, not as psychology in the laboratory sense.

selector.exact: A soul-image is never anything but the image of one quite definite soul

annotations.anchor: A soul-image is never anything but the image of one quite definite soul
annotations.note: Spengler's key term here: the 'soul-image' (Seelenbild) is the picture a Culture forms of its own inner life — its model of what a self is. It is never neutral or universal; it is 'the image of one quite definite soul', the soul of that Culture. So every system of psychology is really a self-portrait of its age, and the soul-images of the Cultures differ as much as their mathematics or their art.

selector.exact: a name, a prime-word like God

annotations.note: Watch the move, which echoes the chapter on Destiny. 'Will', Spengler says, is no concept the psychologist can define but a 'prime-word like God' — something known with immediate inward certainty and never describable. It is the same gesture by which he placed Destiny beyond Causality: the deepest realities are felt, not analysed. For the Faustian soul, Will is the prime-word; that is why its soul-image is all direction and depth.

annotations.anchor: the Apollinian soul-image — Plato’s biga-team with νοῦς as charioteer
annotations.note: Each Culture pictures the soul in its own form. The Apollinian soul-image is Plato's chariot-team (biga) with Reason as charioteer — a small society of bodily parts in the visible present, exactly as the Classical world is a sum of bounded bodies. Set this beside the Faustian soul-image of a single deep will straining into infinity, and the difference of souls is laid bare in the very diagrams they draw of themselves.

selector.exact: Magian soul-image is a strict dualism of two mysterious substances, Spirit and Soul

annotations.anchor: the Magian soul-image is a strict dualism … Spirit and Soul
annotations.note: The Magian soul-image is a dualism of two substances — Spirit and Soul, light and dark, the divine breath and the lower self — locked together in the world-cavern. This is the inner shape of the Magian world: not the bounded body of the Classical nor the lonely will of the Faustian, but a battleground of two powers, the same dualism that runs through Persian, Gnostic, Christian and Islamic theology.

selector.exact: the soul-image underlying the knowledge is really one’s own soul-image

annotations.anchor: the soul-image underlying the knowledge is really one’s own soul-image
annotations.note: Spengler's claim that all psychology is secretly a Culture's self-portrait is, on H. Stuart Hughes's reading, a prime example of what survives in Spengler — the morphologist's eye for how a whole age expresses itself in its forms of thought, as against the dated forecasting Hughes judged worthless. Hughes treats this culture-bound 'soul-image' as a genuine, if overstated, contribution to the history of ideas rather than as science.

selector.exact: Its searches and solutions are fights with shadows and ghosts

annotations.anchor: Its searches and solutions are fights with shadows and ghosts
annotations.note: A sweeping dismissal worth pausing on. Spengler writes off the whole of experimental psychology as 'fights with shadows and ghosts' — a science with no real object. There is a serious point underneath (introspective categories may indeed be culture-bound), but the verdict is total, and it conveniently clears the ground for his own intuitive 'morphology of the soul'. Weigh how much is insight and how much is a rhetorical scorched-earth.
